name: nick-mode-writing-standard
description: Machine-readable writing standard for Nicholas Hidalgo. Enforces direct, factual, voice-forward prose. Based on Humanizer patterns with additional profile-specific rules.

default.description: Applies to all contexts unless overridden by a sub-profile.

resume.description: Resume bullets, cover letters, LinkedIn. Strictest mode: all bans enforced, metrics required.

email.description: Professional email. Bans sycophancy and filler; allows first-person and contractions.

technical.description: Docs, incident summaries, requirements. Bans promotional language; allows copula freely.

rules.name: ban_em_dash
rules.description: Em dashes (—) are forbidden. Use a comma, period, or semicolon.
rules.suggestion: Replace — with a comma, semicolon, or rewrite the sentence.
rules.example_violation: The result—a 40% latency drop—justified the refactor.
rules.example_fix: The result was a 40% latency drop, which justified the refactor.

rules.name: ban_emojis_in_professional_text
rules.description: Emojis are forbidden in headings and bullet points.
rules.suggestion: Remove the emoji. Let the words do the work.
rules.example_violation: 🚀 Launched new checkout flow in Q3.
rules.example_fix: Launched new checkout flow in Q3.

rules.name: prefer_copula
rules.example_violation: The tool serves as a unified platform for all workflows.
rules.example_fix: The tool is a unified platform for all workflows.

rules.name: ban_ai_vocabulary
rules.description: High-frequency AI vocabulary words are prohibited.
rules.suggestion: Use a plain, specific word. Say what you mean directly.
rules.example_violation: I delve into the intricate interplay of systems.
rules.example_fix: I analyze how the systems interact.

rules.name: ban_sycophantic_openers
rules.description: Sycophantic openers and servile phrases are forbidden.

match.pattern: \b(great question|excellent point|you('re| are) absolutely right|of course!|certainly!|i hope this helps|let me know if you('d| would) like)\b

rules.suggestion: Delete the opener. Start with the actual content.
rules.example_violation: Great question! Here's an overview of the topic.
rules.example_fix: The French Revolution began with fiscal crisis and food shortages in 1789.

rules.name: ban_weasel_words
rules.description: Vague attributions without citations are prohibited.
rules.suggestion: Name the source or remove the claim. Cite specifically or don't cite.
rules.example_violation: Experts believe the approach reduces latency.
rules.example_fix: A 2023 AWS benchmark showed a 38% latency reduction using this approach.

rules.name: ban_promotional_adjectives
rules.description: Promotional adjectives without evidence are forbidden.
rules.suggestion: Replace with a specific, verifiable claim.
rules.example_violation: Drove profound impact across the organization.
rules.example_fix: Reduced onboarding time by 3 weeks across 4 teams.

rules.name: ban_filler_phrases
rules.description: Common filler phrases inflate word count without adding meaning.

phrases: it is important to note that
at the end of the day

rules.suggestion: Compress to the simplest equivalent. 'In order to' → 'To'.
rules.example_violation: In order to achieve this, it is important to note that planning is essential.

rules.name: ban_excessive_hedging
rules.description: Three or more stacked hedge words in a single clause is a violation.
rules.suggestion: Pick one hedge if needed. 'May affect outcomes.' not 'could potentially possibly affect.'
rules.example_violation: It could potentially possibly be argued the policy might affect outcomes.

rules.name: ban_generic_conclusions
rules.description: Vague positive closing statements are forbidden.

match.pattern: \b(the future (looks|is) bright|exciting times (lie|are) ahead|continue(s)? (this|our|the) journey (toward|towards)|a (major|significant) step (forward|in the right direction)|the possibilities are endless|sky.?s the limit)\b

rules.suggestion: End with a concrete next action, metric, or factual statement.
rules.example_violation: The future looks bright as we continue this journey toward excellence.
rules.example_fix: The company plans to open two locations and hire 40 engineers next year.

rules.name: ban_knowledge_cutoff_disclaimers
rules.description: AI knowledge-cutoff language must be removed before publishing.

match.pattern: \b(as of my (last )?(training|knowledge|update)|up to my (last )?training|based on available information|while specific details (are|remain) (limited|scarce|not extensively documented)|it (appears|seems) to have been)\b

rules.suggestion: Replace with a sourced fact or remove the sentence entirely.
rules.example_violation: While specific details are limited based on available information, the company appears to have been founded in the 1990s.
rules.example_fix: The company was founded in 1994, per its SEC filing.

rules.name: require_metric_or_outcome
rules.description: Resume bullets must contain at least one metric, outcome, or concrete deliverable.
rules.suggestion: Add a number, percentage, dollar figure, or concrete outcome to this bullet.
rules.example_violation: Improved the onboarding experience for new engineers.
rules.example_fix: Cut new-engineer ramp time from 6 weeks to 3 weeks by rewriting the onboarding runbook.
